commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Maintainers need to decide the public broker API and bundled-vs-external ownership before an automated repair lane can safely modify this draft.; Needs attention: The diff introduces broker credential transport without using the repository's established resolved-runtime SecretRef contract.; Review finding: [P1] Keep broker prefixes from shadowing native channels — extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts:26-28 - [P1... (sha=0f81a445d08de1b80cffc1332ce23cab68c00e65)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a public channel-broker SDK subpath, a bundled channel-broker plugin, docs, tests, export wiring, package metadata, labeler metadata, and Vitest shard registration.

PR surface: Source +1123, Tests +230, Docs +202, Config +75, Generated 0, Other +14. Total +1644 across 31 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: source inspection shows duplicate target prefixes against a first-match current resolver, raw credential header use, and an unused send signal. I did not run live delivery because this review is read-only and the PR intentionally lacks a real broker provider E2E path.
commands.review_followup: Maintainers need to decide the public broker API and bundled-vs-external ownership before an automated repair lane can safely modify this draft.

target.title: [codex] Add channel broker plugin protocol
target.body:
## Summary

This PR lands the Phase 1 foundation for consolidating chat channel maintenance behind a provider-agnostic channel broker surface.

It adds a new public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` subpath with versioned broker contract types and helpers, plus a bundled `channel-broker` extension that adapts those semantics onto the existing channel message lifecycle. The goal is to let OpenClaw own routing/session/message semantics while external providers own platform mechanics for Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, Matrix, and the long tail.

## What changed

- Added `src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts` with `BrokerInboundEventV1`, `BrokerOutboundRequestV1`, `BrokerReceiptV1`, `BrokerProviderCapabilities`, `BrokerProviderHealth`, and target/request/receipt helpers.
- Added bundled `extensions/channel-broker` plugin with:
  - provider/account config normalization
  - outbound HTTP provider transport
  - receive ack policy wiring
  - durable final send, receipt, and unknown-send reconciliation hooks
  - draft/progress preview and final-edit capabilities
  - logical aliases such as `telegram:`, `discord:`, `slack:`, `whatsapp:`, `signal:`, `imessage:`, and `matrix:`
- Added SDK/export/package/docs wiring for the new public subpath and bundled plugin.
- Added docs for channel users and plugin/provider maintainers.
- Added targeted SDK and extension tests plus Vitest shard registration.

## Scope

This is intentionally the protocol/conformance foundation. It does not migrate native Telegram, Discord, or Slack implementations yet. That should happen in follow-up PRs after the broker contract is reviewed.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: OpenClaw can discover the new bundled `channel-broker` channel from a real checkout and the new SDK helpers produce the provider-facing target/outbound protocol shape.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, branch `feat/channel-broker-protocol`, Node `v25.8.2`, current PR head.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node --import tsx -e 'const { listBundledChannelCatalogEntries } = await import("./src/channels/bundled-channel-catalog-read.ts"); const { normalizeChatChannelId } = await import("./src/channels/ids.ts"); const { buildBrokerConversationTarget, createBrokerOutboundRequest } = await import("./src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts"); const catalog = listBundledChannelCatalogEntries().find((entry) => entry.id === "channel-broker"); const target = buildBrokerConversationTarget({ platform: "Telegram", conversationId: "chat 123", threadId: "topic-7" }); const request = createBrokerOutboundRequest({ requestId: "send-proof-1", providerId: "synthetic-provider", platform: "Telegram", accountId: "default", conversation: { id: "chat 123", type: "channel", threadId: "topic-7" }, mode: "final", payloads: [{ text: "broker proof" }], requirements: { text: true, thread: true } }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ discoveredChannel: catalog && { id: catalog.id, order: catalog.order, docsPath: catalog.channel.docsPath }, channelNormalization: normalizeChatChannelId("channel-broker"), target, outboundProtocolVersion: request.version, outboundMode: request.mode, outboundPlatform: request.platform, outboundConversation: request.conversation, outboundPayloadText: request.payloads[0]?.text }, null, 2));'`
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the real checkout:

```json
{
  "discoveredChannel": {
    "id": "channel-broker",
    "order": 60,
    "docsPath": "/channels/channel-broker"
  },
  "channelNormalization": "channel-broker",
  "target": "telegram:chat%20123?threadId=topic-7",
  "outboundProtocolVersion": 1,
  "outboundMode": "final",
  "outboundPlatform": "telegram",
  "outboundConversation": {
    "id": "chat 123",
    "type": "channel",
    "threadId": "topic-7"
  },
  "outboundPayloadText": "broker proof"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The real bundled channel catalog discovers `channel-broker`, normal channel id lookup resolves it, the Telegram-style broker target string preserves topic/thread scope, and the outbound request normalizes to protocol version `1` with platform `telegram` for provider delivery.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end delivery through a third-party broker provider and migration of native Telegram/Discord/Slack adapters; those are Phase 2 follow-up work.

## Validation

Local validation from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`:

- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts`
- `node scripts/sync-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs --check`
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `pnpm lint:extensions:no-src-outside-plugin-sdk`
- `pnpm lint:extensions:no-plugin-sdk-internal`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts src/plugins/bundled-plugin-metadata.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm docs:check-links`

Known broader gates that are red on clean current `upstream/main` before this PR diff:

- `pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check` fails existing SDK surface budgets (`public entrypoints 315 > 303`, `public exports 9094 > 8449`, etc.; with this PR it becomes one public entrypoint larger).
- `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` fails existing unrelated changed-doc label entries. This PR adds the Channel Broker glossary entries it introduces.
target.author: 100yenadmin
